items.title: Ban on live sheep trade has another knock-on effect on WA wool

children.content: Elders is relocating its Western Australian wool handling and marketing operations to Victoria in response to declining wool production volumes in WA.

items.title: Three dead after car hits tree in Perth Hills

children.content: Three people have died after a vehicle left the road and hit a tree in the Perth Hills suburb of Stoneville.

image.altText: A tree in Stoneville

items.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos Islands walked back after backlash

children.content: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

image.altText: White surf crashes against a seawall of stacked sand bags, with tropical plants and coconut trees in the background.

items.title: 'They're not just big fluffy puppy dogs': Breeder warning over Highland cattle popularity

children.content: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

gallery.altText: A tan highland cow with its nose very close to the camera.

items.title: UK man jailed for abusing girl may face renewed visa cancellation

children.content: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

image.altText: A swing at an Adelaide playground site.

items.title: Bones found on Rottnest Island are Indigenous remains

children.content: Several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island this week are historic Indigenous remains, WA Police say.

image.altText: Police holding guard around a fence

items.title: Man who killed foster brother to stay in jail due to lack of mental facilities

children.content: Ian Brooke was found not guilty of murder due to his mental impairment, but will have to stay in jail because of a lack of suitable mental health care facilities to house him.

gallery.altText: A cul-de-sac is cordoned off by police

thumbnail.altText: A cul-de-sac is cordoned off by police

items.title: Hollingsworth 'moving on' from DQ then reinstatement as national champion

children.content: Claudia Hollingsworth insists she ran a fair race, and wants to move on from the drama surrounding the women's 1,500 metres final which saw her disqualified then reinstated, as Jess Hull crashed out.

image.altText: Claudia Hollingsworth has a distressed look on her face as she runs towards the finish

items.title: Hawks end Bulldogs unbeaten run in brilliant Gather Round performance

children.content: Hawthorn delivers a brilliant Gather Round performance to end the Bulldogs unbeaten run, after the Swans beat the Suns at Norwood Oval. Follow live.

image.altText: A Hawthorn AFL forward pumps his fist in celebration after a goal.

items.title: Fans living it up in Adelaide for Gather Round but future of event uncertain

children.content: Occupancy rates and food and drink sales are up on last year's Gather Round, says the Australian Hotels Association, despite concerns fuel prices would deter interstate visitors.

image.altText: People in different club attires walk on a footbridge with Gather Round signs. A lit up Adelaide Oval in the distance at night

items.title: Country primary school staggered by 'routine' $100k water bills

children.content: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

image.altText: The entrance at Dunsborough Primary School

items.title: Warning system made WA community feel 'unsafe' during ex-Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Residents in the Pilbara raise concerns over the state's warning system for cyclones following ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle last month.

items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: The man's
permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

image.altText: The silhouette of a woman sitting, bending over with head resting on clasped hands

items.title: Fixing WA's three-bin problem 'not rocket science' say locals, leaders

children.content: With the three-bin FOGO system on shaky ground outside Perth, some locals are urging the WA government to step up and fund a regional processing plant.

image.altText: Piles of dirt with bits of rubbish throughout

items.title: Collard's extraordinary AFL disciplinary hearing sets new precedent

children.content: In an AFL first, St Kilda's Lance Collard has faced a disciplinary tribunal hearing for conduct unbecoming.

image.altText: GettyImages-2231737279

items.title: Could a buried car be the key to a 35-year-old hit and run mystery?

children.content: Mark Gregory was just 28 when he was killed by a hit-and-run driver while walking home from a party in 1991. His family just wants answers.

image.altText: A wide shot showing a cross

items.title: Kennedy becomes first Australian to break 10-second barrier at home

children.content: Lachlan Kennedy has scorched to a sub-10-second run in the heats of the 100 metres at the Australian Athletics Championships, and promises his best is still to come.

items.title: Convenience store crackdown to curb 'under the counter' illegal nicotine sales

children.content: The City of Fremantle voted unanimously at Wednesday's council meeting to amend local planning laws, in a bid to stop more convenience stores that sell illicit tobacco products from opening.

image.altText: A collection of illegally sold vapes

items.title: Alcoa rejects mercury emissions concerns from its WA refinery

children.content: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

image.altText: A large concrete office building with glass windows on a cloudy day, with Alcoa written in large letters.

items.title: War hero's looming prosecution divides opinion

children.content: Once celebrated as Australia's most decorated living war hero, the looming prosecution of Ben Roberts-Smith is shaping up to be the next trial of the century.

image.altText: a man leaning against a wall which holds a portrait of him wearing an army uniform

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: WA Police launch probe after video shows officer shoving woman to ground during arrest

children.content: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: A classic poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Debra Adelaide's best friend died by suicide. She wrote a novel about her

children.content: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

image.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

items.title: UK charity sues Prince Harry for defamation

children.content: Prince Harry co-founded Sentebale 20 years ago in honour of his late mother, Princess Diana, but now the organisation is suing him for defamation.

image.altText: Prince Harry smiles in front of Sentebale branding, wearing a khaki button-up shirt

items.title: McIlroy's 'just keep swinging' approach takes him to huge Masters lead

children.content: Jason Day does not do a lot wrong in the second round at Augusta National, but Rory McIlroy is looking just about untouchable after two rounds of the Masters.

image.altText: Fans watch Rory McIlroy tee off in the second round of the Masters.

items.title: 'Cosplay is not consent': The rules of dressing up for conventions

children.content: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman dressed in costume
